title: Radiant Italian Polenta Terrine
description: A creamy, glassy polenta terrine layered with seasonal vegetables and finished with a buttery ghee glaze. Served in a terracotta dish, it delivers a cozy, macrobiotic Italian dinner.
instructions: Rinse the cornmeal under cold water to remove dust, then drain.
In a heavy saucepan, bring the water, broth, and a pinch of salt to a rolling boil.
Gradually whisk in the polenta, reduce heat to low, and cook, stirring constantly, for 30 minutes until thick and glossy.
During the last 10 minutes, fold in the grated carrot and zucchini, then stir in the ghee and pepper until melted and combined.
Remove the pot from heat, add the chopped basil and half the Parmesan, stirring until the cheese is melted.
Pour the mixture into a greased terracotta terrine, smoothing the surface with a spatula.
Let the terrine cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours to set into a firm, glassy texture.
Before serving, sprinkle the remaining Parmesan on top and drizzle a thin line of ghee in the center to mimic nigiri shaping.
Slice the terrine into 4-6 wedges and serve chilled or at room temperature.
